Key.markdownDescription:
---

Required: No  
Type: String  
Minimum Length: 1  
Maximum Length: 128  
Update requires: No interruption

Value.markdownDescription:
---

Required: No  
Type: String  
Maximum Length: 256  
Update requires: No interruption

AccessPointTag.markdownDescription:
---

Required: No  
Update requires: No interruption

OwnerUid.description: Specifies the POSIX user ID to apply to the RootDirectory. Accepts values from 0 to 2^32 (4294967295).
OwnerUid.markdownDescription:
Specifies the POSIX user ID to apply to the RootDirectory. Accepts values from 0 to 2^32 (4294967295).

---

Required: Yes  
Type: String  
Pattern: ^[0-9]+$  
Update requires: No interruption

OwnerGid.description: Specifies the POSIX group ID to apply to the RootDirectory. Accepts values from 0 to 2^32 (4294967295).
OwnerGid.markdownDescription:
Specifies the POSIX group ID to apply to the RootDirectory. Accepts values from 0 to 2^32 (4294967295).

---

Required: Yes  
Type: String  
Pattern: ^[0-9]+$  
Update requires: No interruption

Permissions.description: Specifies the POSIX permissions to apply to the RootDirectory, in the format of an octal number representing the file's mode bits.
Permissions.markdownDescription:
Specifies the POSIX permissions to apply to the RootDirectory, in the format of an octal number representing the file's mode bits.

---

Required: Yes  
Type: String  
Pattern: ^[0-7]{3,4}$  
Update requires: No interruption

CreationPermissions.markdownDescription:
---

Required: No  
Update requires: No interruption

Path.description: Specifies the path on the EFS file system to expose as the root directory to NFS clients using the access point to access the EFS file system. A path can have up to four subdirectories. If the specified path does not exist, you are required to provide the CreationPermissions.
Path.markdownDescription:
Specifies the path on the EFS file system to expose as the root directory to NFS clients using the access point to access the EFS file system. A path can have up to four subdirectories. If the specified path does not exist, you are required to provide the CreationPermissions.

---

Required: No  
Type: String  
Minimum Length: 1  
Maximum Length: 100  
Pattern: ^(\/|(\/(?!\.)+[^$#<>;;`|&?{}^*/\n]+){1,4})$  
Update requires: No interruption

CreationPermissions.description: (Optional) Specifies the POSIX IDs and permissions to apply to the access point's RootDirectory. If the RootDirectory>Path specified does not exist, EFS creates the root directory using the CreationPermissions settings when a client connects to an access point. When specifying the CreationPermissions, you must provide values for all properties.   If you do not provide CreationPermissions and the specified RootDirectory>Path does not exist, attempts to mount the file system using the access point will fail.
CreationPermissions.markdownDescription:
(Optional) Specifies the POSIX IDs and permissions to apply to the access point's RootDirectory. If the RootDirectory>Path specified does not exist, EFS creates the root directory using the CreationPermissions settings when a client connects to an access point. When specifying the CreationPermissions, you must provide values for all properties.   If you do not provide CreationPermissions and the specified RootDirectory>Path does not exist, attempts to mount the file system using the access point will fail. 

---

Required: No  
Type:   
Update requires: No interruption

Uid.description: The POSIX user ID used for all file system operations using this access point.
Uid.markdownDescription:
The POSIX user ID used for all file system operations using this access point.

---

Required: Yes  
Type: String  
Pattern: ^[0-9]+$  
Update requires: No interruption

Gid.markdownDescription:
The POSIX group ID used for all file system operations using this access point.

---

Required: Yes  
Type: String  
Pattern: ^[0-9]+$  
Update requires: No interruption

SecondaryGids.description: Secondary POSIX group IDs used for all file system operations using this access point.

items.markdownDescription:
---

Required: No  
Type: String  
Pattern: ^[0-9]+$  
Update requires: No interruption

SecondaryGids.markdownDescription:
Secondary POSIX group IDs used for all file system operations using this access point.

Required: No  
Type: Array

AccessPointId.markdownDescription:
---

Required: No  
Type: String  
Maximum Length: 256  
Pattern: ^(arn:aws[-a-z]*:s3files:[0-9a-z-:]+:file-system/fs-[0-9a-f]{17,40}/access-point/fsap-[0-9a-f]{17,40}|fsap-[0-9a-f]{17,40})$  
Update requires: No interruption
Read only property: Yes

AccessPointArn.markdownDescription:
---

Required: No  
Type: String  
Maximum Length: 256  
Pattern: ^arn:aws[-a-z]*:s3files:[0-9a-z-:]+:file-system/fs-[0-9a-f]{17,40}/access-point/fsap-[0-9a-f]{17,40}$  
Update requires: No interruption
Read only property: Yes

ClientToken.description: (optional) A string of up to 64 ASCII characters that Amazon EFS uses to ensure idempotent creation.
ClientToken.markdownDescription:
(optional) A string of up to 64 ASCII characters that Amazon EFS uses to ensure idempotent creation.

---

Required: No  
Type: String  
Minimum Length: 1  
Maximum Length: 64  
Pattern: ^(.+)$  
Update requires: Replacement

Type: Array  
Update requires: No interruption

FileSystemId.description: The ID of the S3 Files file system that the access point provides access to.
FileSystemId.markdownDescription:
The ID of the S3 Files file system that the access point provides access to.

---

Required: Yes  
Type: String  
Maximum Length: 128  
Pattern: ^(arn:aws[-a-z]*:s3files:[0-9a-z-:]+:file-system/fs-[0-9a-f]{17,40}|fs-[0-9a-f]{17,40})$  
Update requires: Replacement

PosixUser.description: The operating system user and group applied to all compute drive requests made using the access point.
PosixUser.markdownDescription:
The operating system user and group applied to all compute drive requests made using the access point.

---

Required: No  
Type:   
Update requires: Replacement

RootDirectory.description: Specifies the directory on the Amazon EFS file system that the access point exposes as the root directory of your file system to NFS clients using the access point. The clients using the access point can only access the root directory and below. If the RootDirectory>Path specified does not exist, EFS creates it and applies the CreationPermissions settings when a client connects to an access point. When specifying a RootDirectory, you need to provide the Path, and the CreationPermissions is optional.
RootDirectory.markdownDescription:
Specifies the directory on the Amazon EFS file system that the access point exposes as the root directory of your file system to NFS clients using the access point. The clients using the access point can only access the root directory and below. If the RootDirectory>Path specified does not exist, EFS creates it and applies the CreationPermissions settings when a client connects to an access point. When specifying a RootDirectory, you need to provide the Path, and the CreationPermissions is optional.

---

Required: No  
Type:   
Update requires: Replacement
